candidate_llm_application: KATAJJAQ-BREAK: two-clone alternating self-play. Clones A,B from same checkpoint; shared anchor prompt; alternate N-token turns conditioned on opponent's last segment; break detector monitors (i) premature EOS, (ii) format-violation/hallucination, (iii) verbatim-repeat (n-gram-overlap > theta). On first break by X, terminate with reward(X)=-1, reward(Y)=+1; PPO update.
rule: hit iff kw_count>=2 OR sem_score>=0.7 OR func_score>=0.7; total_hits>=1 => FAIL

results.title: SPC 2504.19162 — sneaky-generator vs critic adversarial-game RL
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN family. SPC employs two-agent adversarial game between sneaky-generator and critic with role-asymmetric reward attribution; identical in mechanism to KATAJJAQ-BREAK's two-clone alternation with asymmetric break-failure attribution. Both alternate turns conditioned on opponent's output, both attribute terminal reward to the failing agent. Cultural framing of katajjaq is the only differentiator; computational core is the same self-play-with-attribution scheme.

results.title: Code-A1 2603.15611 — decoupled code+test adversarial RL + Mistake Book
results.rationale: Decoupled code+test adversarial coevolution with explicit Mistake Book mechanism IS break-event-driven RL: the test that exposes a mistake is functionally the break-detector, and the asymmetric penalty against the failing side mirrors KATAJJAQ's loss-on-break attribution. Two-agent alternation + failure-event terminal reward already covered.

results.title: Multi-Agent Evolve 2510.23595 — Proposer+Solver+Judge coevolution
results.rationale: Tri-agent Proposer-Solver-Judge coevolution subsumes two-clone alternating duel with break-detector; Judge plays the break-detector role and asymmetric attribution to Solver-on-failure is identical to KATAJJAQ-BREAK's reward(X)=-1 on break. Form is adversarial-coevolution with role-asymmetric penalty already standard.

results.title: Digital Red Queen (Sakana) — self-play warriors defeat all prior
results.rationale: Digital Red Queen self-play with binary-loss tournament attribution is the canonical reference for two-clone winner-loser asymmetric reward. KATAJJAQ-BREAK's reward(X)=-1, reward(Y)=+1 on first-to-break is exactly DRQ's tournament outcome attribution at the round level. Self-play coevolution with binary terminal attribution already canonical.

results.title: EOS Decision Hallucination 2402.14545 — hallucination tied to EOS timing
results.rationale: The premature-EOS-as-failure-signal AND hallucination-as-failure-signal are exactly the two failure mechanisms KATAJJAQ-BREAK enumerates as break-events (i) and (ii). EOS-hallucination paper already operationalizes the EOS-timing-as-failure measurement and provides the detector primitive. Two of three KATAJJAQ break-detector components are anticipated here.

results.title: Behaviorally Calibrated RL 2512.19920 — RLVR rewards encourage guessing; terminal reward calibration
results.rationale: Terminal-reward calibration with failure-attribution penalty is the same RLVR mechanism KATAJJAQ-BREAK uses: PPO against terminal-only reward where the breaking agent receives -1. Behaviorally-Calibrated RL operationalizes precisely the asymmetric terminal penalty for failure-events. PPO + terminal break-event reward already canonical.

results.title: Reasoning Hallucination 2505.23646 — flaw-repetition + think-answer mismatch
results.rationale: Flaw-repetition (verbatim/n-gram repeat of prior content) + think-answer mismatch is exactly the (iii) verbatim-repeat detector AND (ii) format-violation detector of KATAJJAQ-BREAK. The repetition-as-failure-signal is already operationalized; n-gram-overlap threshold detection is standard. Two more of three break-detector components anticipated.

results.title: Adversarial Preference Learning Findings ACL 2025 — attacker-defender alternation
results.rationale: Attacker-defender alternation with asymmetric role-based reward is structurally identical to KATAJJAQ's two-clone duel with break-attribution. Adversarial preference learning's alternating-turn formulation + role-asymmetric loss already covers the two-clone alternating game. Cultural framing (katajjaq) supplies narrative, not mechanism.

reason: All 8 results trigger hit under sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7. SPC 2504.19162 is the exact functional twin: two-agent adversarial RL game (sneaky-generator vs critic) with role-asymmetric terminal attribution is identical in computational substance to KATAJJAQ-BREAK's two-clone alternating duel with asymmetric break-failure penalty. The three break-detectors are individually anticipated: (i) premature-EOS by EOS Decision Hallucination 2402.14545, (ii) format-violation/hallucination by Reasoning Hallucination 2505.23646 + EOS Hallucination 2402.14545, (iii) verbatim-repeat by Reasoning Hallucination 2505.23646 (flaw-repetition). The PPO + terminal-only reward is exactly Behaviorally Calibrated RL 2512.19920's setting. Two-agent / multi-agent alternation with asymmetric attribution is jointly covered by SPC, Code-A1, Multi-Agent Evolve, Digital Red Queen, and Adversarial Preference Learning. The katajjaq cultural framing supplies a novel narrative (throat-singing duel, run-out-of-breath, first-to-laugh) but no novel computational mechanism beyond self-play + break-event detector + asymmetric terminal reward already canonical. total_hits=8 >= 1, candidate FAILS the threshold.
recommendation: REJECT candidate. The proposed two-clone alternating self-play with asymmetric break-failure penalty is fully anticipated by SPC 2504.19162 (two-agent adversarial RL with role-asymmetric reward) + Code-A1 2603.15611 (decoupled adversarial code+test) + Multi-Agent Evolve 2510.23595 (tri-agent coevolution with judge-as-detector) + Digital Red Queen (binary tournament attribution) + EOS Hallucination 2402.14545 (EOS-timing detector) + Reasoning Hallucination 2505.23646 (flaw-repetition + format-mismatch detectors) + Behaviorally Calibrated RL 2512.19920 (terminal-only PPO calibration) + Adversarial Preference Learning (attacker-defender alternation). To salvage, candidate would need (a) a distinctive break-event class not reducible to EOS/format/repeat (e.g., rhythmic-cycle-mismatch with phase-locked coupling between clones where break is defined relative to a third joint-rhythm latent), (b) breath-budget as an explicit token-economy resource that depletes nonlinearly with utterance complexity and must be replenished by silence, or (c) audience-laugh-detector as a separate trained third agent whose verdict (not n-gram threshold) determines break; none of these are present in the current proposal, which is competence-asymmetric two-agent self-play with rule-based break detector — already standard.
